Bead: agent-6c7a4c11 — Harness × Model Matrix Benchmark (initial tranche)

Status: no_changes (epic — needs child-bead breakdown, not monolithic execution)

## What this bead actually is

Per the governing plan at docs/research/harness-matrix-plan-2026-04-29.md
(v7, codex-reviewed), this bead is the EPIC bead (referred to as NEW1 in the
plan's bead breakdown). The plan's exit criterion is "Filing of epic bead
(bead-breakdown §NEW1)" — i.e. the planning doc closes once the epic is
filed. It does NOT say the epic itself is satisfied by a single
implementation pass.

The plan's bead breakdown (lines 408-429) explicitly enumerates ~18 child
beads under this epic:

  NEW2   Author SD-010 (NEW spec)
  NEW3   Amend SD-008 §6 (multi-harness)
  NEW4   Amend SD-009 §5/§7/§9
  NEW5   Amend FEAT-005 (token streams)
  NEW6   Step 0 — model census (no code; user sign-off)
  NEW7   Step 1 — submodule init + egress canary (smoke model)
  NEW8   Step 2 — profile schema + Go loader
  NEW9   Step 3 — adapter base + ddx-agent + noop + dumb_script
  NEW10  Step 4a — ddx-agent-bench matrix subcommand
  NEW11  Step 4b — matrix-aggregate subcommand
  NEW12  Step 5 — cost-cap observation + guardrails
  NEW13  Step 6 — canary subset manifest
  NEW14  Step 7 — OSS harness install spike (pi, opencode)
  NEW15  Step 8a — pi adapter
  NEW16  Step 8b — opencode adapter
  NEW17        — CI: adapter unit tests on PR
  NEW18        — anchor profile YAML (post-NEW6)
  NEW19  Step 9 — Phase A.1 matrix run + memo

The plan's text says explicitly: "Closes when NEW19 (Phase A.1 memo) is
filed." NEW19 is the leaf child bead, not this epic.

## Why this cannot be executed in a single pass

The bead's stated acceptance — "Phase A.1 memo at
docs/research/matrix-baseline-phase-a1-2026-XX-XX.md lands; all 27 runs
reach a terminal final_status; ≥24/27 are graded_*; cost reconciled to
observation; caveat block per SD-010" — requires every one of the
prerequisites below, none of which are satisfiable inside this isolated
execution worktree:

1. **SD-010 must exist and be Approved.** The plan (line 361) states:
   "specs lead implementation; do not start Step 2 without SD-010 in
   Status: Approved." SD-010 has not been authored yet
   (docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/ does not contain it). Authoring
   a normative spec covering D1–D6, profile schema, adapter contract, the
   11-status failure taxonomy, aggregator format, caveat policy, and ToS
   policy — and then getting codex peer review + user sign-off — is a
   self-contained bead (NEW2), not a sub-step of this one.

2. **User sign-off on the anchor model** (NEW6 census). The plan's Step 0
   requires a one-page model census, applied selection-fallback hierarchy,
   and explicit user sign-off on the (anchor, second-model) pair. An
   automated bead pass cannot fabricate that sign-off.

3. **Harbor + Docker + TB-2 submodule + paid OpenRouter API access.**
   Phase A.1 is 27 actual paid runs against the upstream Harbor container
   with a real provider model in the $1–3/Mtok bracket. The execution
   environment here has no paid API key surfaced, no Docker daemon
   committed for benchmark spend, and the TB-2 submodule is uninitialized
   (plan line 79). A "no_changes" pass cannot run them.

4. **OSS harnesses pi and opencode must be installed in-container** with
   pinned versions, non-interactive flags, license review, and adapter
   wiring (NEW14 + NEW15 + NEW16).

5. **Cost-cap observation runs (NEW12)** must be done before A.1 — itself
   a paid one-shot run.

In short: the acceptance criterion for this bead is downstream of ~18
prerequisite child beads, several of which require human decisions and
paid provider spend.

## What is done

- The plan (docs/research/harness-matrix-plan-2026-04-29.md, v7) is
  written, codex-reviewed, and committed at base-rev 2e59a45.
- This bead (the epic) is filed (it is the bead being executed now).

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Do NOT escalate this bead to a stronger model — escalation will not change
the structural problem (the work is gated on user sign-off + paid spend +
~18 prerequisite child beads).

Instead, the orchestrator should:

1. **File the child beads NEW2–NEW19** as separate work items, with the
   `depends_on` graph documented in plan §"Dependency / ordering rules"
   (lines 444-457). Each child bead has its own deterministic acceptance
   criterion in the plan's "Bead acceptance criteria" table (lines
   464-482).

2. **Close this epic bead with reason=epic_decomposed** (or similar) once
   the child beads are filed. The epic's "completion" is the cumulative
   closure of NEW19, not direct execution of the epic body itself.

3. Per the bead's own escape clause: "If A.1 cannot meet criteria,
   root-cause beads are filed in the follow-up epic and this epic is
   closed with reason=blocked rather than retried indefinitely." That
   clause anticipates exactly this situation — epics close on
   decomposition or on blocked, not on a single execute-bead pass.

## Files touched in this pass

None outside .ddx/executions/. No commit produced.
